reasoning: The current effective yield of the ICE BofA Euro High Yield Index is approximately 5.06 (as of Dec 5, 2025), which is near the lower boundary of its 1-year range (which saw a low of ~4.92 and a high of ~6.15). Recent macroeconomic reports from early December indicate that Euro zone inflation has ticked up, leading markets to price out ECB rate cuts in the near term (with the deposit rate steady at 2%). With risk-free rates likely to be held steady, and yields already quite low for the high-yield sector, there is asymmetric upside risk for the yield from this 5.06 baseline. Although the yield dropped from 5.12 to 5.06 over the past week, suggesting some near-term downward momentum (likely due to credit spread tightening), typical mean-reverting behavior of index yields near their lower bounds gives a higher than 50% probability of an increase. As the forecast horizon extends, accumulated random-walk variance dilutes this initial mean-reverting edge, so probabilities regress smoothly toward 0.5 over time.
